reasoning: NOAA's National Centers for Environmental Information (NCEI) explicitly calculated a 4% probability that 2025 will be the hottest year on record as of their February 2025 assessment. The UK Met Office similarly forecasts that 2025 will be the third-warmest year, trailing 2024 and 2023. The transition to a La Niña climate pattern is currently underway and typically leads to global cooling relative to El Niño years (such as 2024). While January 2025 was exceptionally warm for global land, February 2025 ranked only 3rd warmest globally. Given the strong consensus from major climate agencies and the known cooling impact of La Niña, the chance of 2025 setting a new record is very low, aligning perfectly with the NOAA statistical forecast of 4%.
